steps.text: Activate CDC’s Emergency Operations Center to manage the agency’s Zika response.
Form teams to focus expertise on different areas of the complex response.
Award funds to support local responses to protect Americans from Zika.
Deploy teams of experts to assist in public health responses in affected areas.
Work closely with international partners to learn more about Zika.
Issue an advisory to alert US healthcare providers and the public about Zika.
Encourage US healthcare providers to report suspected Zika cases to public health departments.
Conduct epidemiological investigations and report confirmed Zika cases to CDC.
Provide travel advisories to pregnant women regarding travel to areas with Zika.
Develop a diagnostic test protocol for Zika and distribute it to laboratories.
Focus on prevention and control measures for mosquito bites and sexual transmission.
Support local mosquito control efforts through guidance and resources.
Establish collaborations with the US Olympic Committee to protect travelers and athletes from Zika.
Provide Zika prevention information and guidance to Olympic athletes.
